seed_problem: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight; checker validates capacities and scores total value / baseline.
mutation: composite: generalize the input from a single value-per-item to a full per-agent (telescope) cost/value matrix, and tighten the output so each item (target) picks at most one capacitated agent -> a generalized assignment problem (multiple-knapsack + weighted matching), NP-hard, where value-greedy, value-per-minute greedy, and reassignment local search diverge.
title: Aperture Ledger: Telescope Array Observing Schedule
strategies: first-fit baseline: assign each target in input order to the first telescope with room (value-blind reference)
value-greedy: order targets by their best value, place each on the highest-value telescope that still fits
value-per-minute greedy: sort all (target, telescope) pairs by val/cost and assign feasibly for capacity efficiency
reassignment local search: seed from a greedy schedule then hill-climb via relocation and evict-and-swap moves that strictly raise total value
randomized multi-restart: perturb the greedy ordering across seeded restarts and keep the highest-value feasible schedule
